title: Robert Parish: Talk of Celtics trading Jaylen Brown is ‘disturbing’
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Celtics legend Robert Parish criticizes potential trade of Jaylen Brown, calling it a serious miscalculation by management.
Jaylen Brown was initially part of a failed trade involving Bucks star Giannis Antetokounmpo to the Miami Heat.
The Celtics face challenges trading Brown as his market value reportedly requires lowering their initial asking price.

title: State Police sergeant, former lieutenant under criminal investigation for alleged coverup in wake of fatal crash, court documents reveal
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Lt. Anthony DeLucia and Sgt. Mark Delaney are under criminal investigation related to the 2023 crash involving Sgt. Scott Quigley.
The investigation revealed allegations of DeLucia knowing Quigley's intoxication and advising not to disclose it.
Following the investigation, DeLucia retired and was dishonorably discharged; Delaney is on restricted duty.

title: 5 players Bruins should target in free agency on Wednesday
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Bruins have $7.7 million in cap space as they look to strengthen their roster in free agency.
Possible targets for Boston include defenseman Ryan Shea and forward Matias Maccelli, both considered solid value options.
Veteran defenseman Jacob Trouba is suggested as a possible short-term defensive improvement for the Bruins.

title: Watch: Red Sox, Nationals brawl after Willson Contreras charges mound
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox's Willson Contreras charged the mound after an exchange with Nationals pitcher Cade Cavalli at Fenway Park.
Three players, including Contreras, were ejected from the game following the on-field altercation between the teams.
Red Sox interim manager Chad Tracy was also ejected after the brawl, arguing with umpires post-scrum.

title: Person shot and killed, suspect also dead after incident that closed I-495 in Lowell for hours, officials say
author: Darin Zullo and Samantha Genzer
bullets: A person was killed, and the suspect died from self-inflicted wounds during a police standoff on I-495 in Lowell.
The incident began with reports of shots fired at Highland Plaza, prompting Berlin police response and followed by a police chase.
I-495 was closed for hours Tuesday afternoon, diverting traffic at Exits 89 and 92, reopening fully by 10 p.m.

title: Dunkin’ drops new ‘Eagle Cup’ for America’s 250th
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Dunkin' introduced a $10.99 'Eagle Cup' featuring a sculpted bald eagle head lid for Independence Day and America's 250th anniversary.
The purchase includes a medium beverage and a 30-day discount on certain drinks, redeemable until July 31.
The limited-edition cup is available nationwide at participating Dunkin' locations, with only 20 per store.

title: NBA insider: Celtics must ‘significantly’ lower asking price in Jaylen Brown trade
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Celtics demand four first-round picks plus players for Jaylen Brown, causing trade difficulties.
Despite MVP-level season, Brown's trade prospects challenge Celtics due to high asking price.
Portland Trail Blazers and Denver Nuggets show limited interest in acquiring Jaylen Brown.

title: 3 injured in Theater District shooting
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Three men were shot in Boston's Theater District at 216 Tremont St., suffering non-life-threatening injuries, on Monday night.
Boston Police responded to the scene around 10:50 p.m., but no arrests have been made as the investigation continues.
Authorities have not released the identities of the victims, and the public is urged to call 617-343-4571 with information.

title: Healey vows urgent response as Haverhill sewage spill fouls Merrimack River
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Gov. Maura Healey coordinates emergency response to 8 million-gallon sewage spill into Merrimack River from Haverhill.
Beaches in Ipswich, Newburyport, Essex, and Salisbury closed to swimmers; shellfish areas temporarily shut in several towns.
Massachusetts officials and Haverhill work on bypass pipe installation to redirect waste; testing for water safety continues.

title: Bruins ‘comfortable’ taking a leap of faith on two goalie prospects in 2026 NHL Draft
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins drafted goalies Yuri Ivanov and Roberto Henriquez in the 2026 NHL Draft, aiming to bolster their goaltending prospects.
Yuri Ivanov, selected 56th overall, impressed with a .924 save percentage in Russia's junior league and will remain in Russia for development.
Roberto Henriquez, joining Boston College, showed promise with a .921 save percentage in the USHL, enhancing the Bruins' goaltending depth.
